components.rationale: Ollama is the lowest-friction local model manager for commodity hardware; it supports all targeted model families (qwen2.5-coder, deepseek-coder-v2) via a stable REST API. All inference calls route through the internal_inference_gateway adapter; Ollama is never called directly from stage or manager code.
components.activation_condition: always active; no additional evidence required
components.removal_strategy: Replace with vLLM or another POSIX-compatible backend only if Ollama becomes unavailable on target hardware; update internal_inference_gateway routing table; no caller changes required.

components.rationale: vLLM provides higher-throughput batch inference for larger models when GPU resources are available. It is optional because Ollama satisfies the default path; vLLM is only activated when OLLAMA_API_BASE_32B points to a vLLM endpoint or explicitly enabled in routing policy.
components.activation_condition: Activate when OLLAMA_API_BASE_32B routes to a vLLM-backed endpoint or when benchmark evidence shows Ollama throughput is the bottleneck for large-model runs.
components.removal_strategy: Remove routing entry from internal_inference_gateway when GPU resources are not available; fall back to Ollama for all model families.

components.rationale: OpenHands defines mature workspace isolation and tool invocation patterns that are reusable as reference implementations. Only SDK patterns and components are adopted, not the full execution runtime, because the repo owns its own orchestration layer and promotion pipeline.
components.activation_condition: Activate workspace isolation patterns or tool invocation interfaces when the agent_runtime requires workspace boundary enforcement beyond current permission_engine scope.
components.removal_strategy: Extract adopted patterns into repo-owned implementations; remove OpenHands SDK dependency from agent_runtime once patterns are stable.

components.rationale: Aider's RepoMap provides best-in-class symbol extraction, import graph analysis, and multi-file context ranking. It is the default backend for codebase_repomap.py and the AiderExecutor adapter. All calls route through the shared_runtime_adapter_layer; Aider is never invoked as a standalone agent bypassing the promotion pipeline.
components.activation_condition: always active for repomap generation and AiderExecutor code modification tasks; no additional evidence required
components.removal_strategy: Replace with a repo-owned symbol extractor only if Aider's license or interface changes incompatibly; update shared_runtime_adapter_layer; no stage or manager code changes required.

components.rationale: MCP is the standard protocol for exposing tools to LLMs without tight coupling. Adopting MCP as the tool boundary ensures that external tool integrations do not depend on model-specific function-call formats and can be tested independently of the inference layer.
components.activation_condition: Activate when any stage or manager component needs to expose or consume external tools; the tool_interoperability_layer wraps all MCP server/client interactions.
components.removal_strategy: Replace only if a superior interoperability standard supersedes MCP; all tool registrations are isolated in the tool_interoperability_layer.

components.rationale: Qdrant provides production-grade vector search with filtering, payload indexing, and local-first deployment. It is the default semantic memory backend for RAG stages and long-horizon context retrieval. All vector operations route through the retrieval_and_memory_layer adapter.
components.activation_condition: Activate when any RAG stage or memory component requires semantic similarity search beyond BM25 token matching.
components.removal_strategy: Replace with an alternative vector store (e.g., Weaviate, Milvus) only if Qdrant performance or reliability degrades; update retrieval_and_memory_layer adapter; no RAG stage code changes required.

components.rationale: gVisor provides kernel-level syscall interception for sandboxed code execution with low container overhead. It is the default sandbox for all agent-driven code execution because it provides strong isolation without the cold-start penalty of Firecracker VMs. All code execution requests must pass through the sandbox_execution_layer.
components.activation_condition: always required for any code execution path; no additional evidence required
components.removal_strategy: Replace with Firecracker only if evidence shows syscall-level isolation is insufficient for the threat model; conditional upgrade path is already recorded for Firecracker.

components.rationale: SWE-bench is the standard real-world software engineering benchmark for measuring code modification capability. It is the reference harness for the Codex 5.1 replacement milestone and for capability gate advancement. Results feed the learning loop and promotion qualification pipeline via evaluation_wrappers.
components.activation_condition: Activate for Phase 3 capability measurement and ongoing benchmark campaigns; evaluation results must be stored in artifacts/benchmarks/ under the artifact_schema contract.
components.removal_strategy: Supplement with additional benchmarks; SWE-bench is not removed as long as the Codex 5.1 milestone is active.

components.rationale: Backstage provides a unified developer portal and software catalog that enriches context for agent task generation. Approved now; integrate only in Phase 5 when the catalog_context_layer is being built and CMDB enrichment is on the critical path.
components.activation_condition: Integrate only in Phase 5 when catalog context enrichment is a scheduled gate; do not integrate before Phase 5 even if technically feasible.
components.removal_strategy: Replace with a lightweight catalog adapter if Backstage operational overhead exceeds benefit; catalog_context_layer interface remains stable.

components.rationale: GLPI is the default authoritative CMDB providing asset inventory and IT asset management context. Approved now; integrate only in Phase 5 when cmdb_context_layer is a scheduled gate. GLPI is preferred over i-doit because its open-source licensing and REST API are more operationally aligned.
components.activation_condition: Integrate only in Phase 5 when CMDB context enrichment is a scheduled gate; GLPI is default unless rejected by operational constraints.
components.removal_strategy: Fall back to i-doit only if GLPI is operationally rejected; CloudQuery enrichment may supplement either CMDB choice.

components.rationale: CloudQuery provides read-only enrichment of CMDB data from cloud provider APIs. Approved as optional enrichment alongside GLPI in Phase 5; it does not replace GLPI as the authoritative CMDB.
components.activation_condition: Activate alongside GLPI in Phase 5 only when cloud asset enrichment is a scheduled requirement; optional if CMDB is sufficient without cloud-provider sync.
components.removal_strategy: Remove if cloud asset coverage is not required for the active task scope; GLPI remains authoritative regardless.

components.rationale: i-doit is not the default CMDB. GLPI is preferred for its lighter operational footprint and more permissive licensing. i-doit is only reconsidered if GLPI is rejected by operational constraints or if strict ITIL relationship modeling (CI dependency trees, service maps) dominates the Phase 5 requirements. Do not integrate by default.
components.activation_condition: Reconsider only if GLPI is rejected by operational decision or if strict ITIL relationship modeling is demonstrated as a hard gate requirement in Phase 5.
components.removal_strategy: Not adopted as default; no removal required unless previously activated under the conditional path.

components.rationale: Firecracker provides microVM-level isolation for untrusted code execution scenarios where gVisor syscall interception is insufficient. It is conditionally approved for Phase 6+ only if a security review determines that kernel-level isolation is required. gVisor remains the default sandbox.
components.activation_condition: Activate only if a security review provides evidence that gVisor syscall isolation is insufficient for the active threat model and microVM isolation is required. Do not activate before Phase 6.
components.removal_strategy: Not the default path; gVisor remains default unless Firecracker is activated by the conditional evidence trigger.

components.rationale: Temporal provides durable workflow execution with built-in retry, versioning, and long-running state. It is conditionally approved for Phase 6+ only if the manager orchestration layer requires cross-session durability guarantees that the current checkpoint/resume model cannot provide. The current stage7_manager with checkpoint-based resume is the default.
components.activation_condition: Activate only if evidence in Phase 6+ shows that manager-level checkpoint resume is insufficient for cross-session durability requirements and Temporal's operational complexity is justified. Do not activate before Phase 6.
components.removal_strategy: Not the default path; stage7_manager checkpoint model remains default unless Temporal is activated by the conditional evidence trigger.
